contractNotes: This file is the source-of-truth contract for the Obsidian feature vault fixture.
requiredHandling states what the fixture and later verification runs must prove, not marketing claims.
currentProductState is descriptive and intentionally conservative; do not upgrade it without code or manual evidence.

statusDefinitions.render-edit: EweNote should render this in the main note workflow and preserve edits on round trip.
statusDefinitions.preserve-round-trip: EweNote may not render this natively yet, but import/export must preserve the source or file bytes.
statusDefinitions.navigate/search: EweNote should expose this through search, links, backlinks, outline, tags, or related note navigation.
statusDefinitions.manual-only: This contract is validated by manual QA rather than a reliable automated assertion today.
statusDefinitions.out-of-scope: This is intentionally not a parity target for current EweNote product scope.

categories.label: Markdown syntax and source fidelity
fixtureCoverage: Multiple Markdown notes with overlapping syntax so import/export and editor checks can distinguish exact-preserve cases from rich-render cases.
At least one note that intentionally relies on source mode for unsupported or partially rendered syntax.

features.label: Headings, paragraphs, emphasis, blockquotes, lists, and checklist items
Markdown tables
features.notes: Recent parity work added explicit table support.

features.label: Inline code and fenced code blocks
Callouts and footnotes
features.notes: Fixture should prove source fidelity even where rich rendering is incomplete.

features.label: Obsidian comments, highlights, escaped Markdown, and raw HTML
External Markdown links and source-mode fallback for unsupported rich rendering
features.notes: Source mode is valid parity evidence when EweNote intentionally preserves syntax instead of rendering it.

features.label: Math and Mermaid source blocks
features.notes: This run does not claim native diagram or math rendering parity.

categories.label: Properties, tags, and frontmatter-backed metadata
fixtureCoverage: Notes with YAML frontmatter covering scalar, list, boolean, date, alias, cssclasses, and tag-heavy metadata.
Property-bearing notes that later Bases fixtures can reference without inventing separate synthetic metadata.

features.label: YAML frontmatter properties including text, arrays, booleans, and dates
Default Obsidian properties such as tags, aliases, and cssclasses
features.notes: Fixture should cover them even if some are only preserved today.

features.label: Tags and properties participating in note filtering or discovery flows

categories.label: Links, embeds, and navigation surfaces
fixtureCoverage: Notes that link across folders, aliases, headings, blocks, embeds, and intentionally broken targets.
Enough cross-link density to exercise backlinks, outgoing links, outline, quick switcher, and search checks in later runs.

features.label: Wiki links, aliases, and path-based note resolution
Heading references and block references
Backlinks, outgoing links, and unlinked mention discovery
Outline, quick switcher, and full-text search workflows
Embeds of notes, headings, blocks, and attachments inside Markdown

categories.label: Attachments and vault file inventory
fixtureCoverage: Tiny license-clean placeholder binaries for each supported attachment family where practical.
File references from Markdown notes so preserved files are not orphaned test data.

features.label: Documented Obsidian attachment classes: images, audio, video, and PDF
features.notes: The fixture should cover the documented file families, even where import scanning is still incomplete.

features.label: Safe fixture-only .obsidian config files
features.notes: Keep config handling explicit and narrow. Do not broaden import/export to arbitrary app settings without approval.

categories.label: Obsidian Canvas files
fixtureCoverage: At least one valid .canvas file using note, text, file, and web cards.
Linked note and attachment targets that also exist elsewhere in the fixture vault.

features.label: .canvas files stored in the vault using Obsidian's Canvas format
features.notes: EweNote should preserve file inventory and bytes without claiming native Canvas editing UI.

features.label: Manual validation that fixture Canvas files still open in Obsidian

categories.label: Obsidian Bases files and property queries
fixtureCoverage: At least one valid .base file that references frontmatter-backed properties from real fixture notes.
Query definitions that exercise filters, sorts, and a mix of default and custom properties without claiming native EweNote UI.

features.label: .base files stored in the vault for property-backed views and queries
features.notes: EweNote should preserve file inventory and bytes without claiming native Bases querying or view UI.

features.label: Properties referenced by Bases definitions remain present in source notes

categories.label: Folders, duplicate names, and path-sensitive behavior
fixtureCoverage: Nested folders, duplicate filenames in different directories, and path-collision edge cases.
Case, punctuation, and broken-link examples that make navigation assertions concrete.

features.label: Nested folders and stable relative paths
Duplicate note names in different folders
features.notes: Needs fixture coverage and navigation assertions.

features.label: Broken links, case differences, punctuation differences, and title collisions

categories.label: Obsidian core plugin-adjacent note workflows
fixtureCoverage: Daily note, template, slides, bookmark, search-target, and word-count-oriented notes represented as plain vault content.
Fixture content that supports manual workflow checks without implying plugin-surface parity.

features.label: Daily notes and templates as note workflows
features.notes: EweNote can test note content workflows without claiming core-plugin parity.

features.label: Command-palette, slash-command, and quick action workflows that touch note creation or editing
features.notes: These are workflow checks, not a claim of Obsidian command parity.

features.label: Tags view, outline, and word-count style workflows
Bookmarks, saved search targets, and Slides source notes
features.notes: Preserve fixture content without claiming dedicated UI.

categories.label: Explicit non-goals for this parity harness
fixtureCoverage: Optional preserve-only files and notes may reference these surfaces, but the fixture must not be used to imply native EweNote feature delivery.

features.label: Native Canvas editing UI
Native Bases querying and view UI
Graph view, Publish, Obsidian Sync, and community plugin ecosystem parity
Obsidian CLI command parity
